briefdescription.para: Initialize (or reset) a protothread so it starts from the top.
Open a protothread body. Must be the first statement in the protothread function, before any PT_* operations.

detaileddescription.para: Expands to the head of a switch statement that jumps to the last saved continuation point.

briefdescription.para: Close a protothread body. Returns PT_EXITED and resets the continuation so the thread can be restarted if desired.

memberdef.name: PT_WAIT_UNTIL
memberdef.initializer: do { \ (pt)->lc = __LINE__; \ SYN_FALLTHROUGH; /* intentional Duff's device */ \ case __LINE__: \ if (!(cond)) { \ return PT_WAITING; \ } \ } while (0)

briefdescription.para: Block until cond evaluates to true.

detaileddescription.para: Each time the protothread is polled and cond is false, it returns PT_WAITING and resumes at this point on the next call.

briefdescription.para: Block while cond is true. Dual of PT_WAIT_UNTIL.

memberdef.name: PT_YIELD
memberdef.initializer: do { \ _pt_yield_flag = 0; \ (pt)->lc = __LINE__; \ SYN_FALLTHROUGH; \ case __LINE__: \ if (_pt_yield_flag == 0) { \ return PT_YIELDED; \ } \ } while (0)

briefdescription.para: Yield control unconditionally. The protothread will resume at this point on the next call.

memberdef.name: PT_YIELD_UNTIL
memberdef.initializer: do { \ _pt_yield_flag = 0; \ (pt)->lc = __LINE__; \ SYN_FALLTHROUGH; \ case __LINE__: \ if ((_pt_yield_flag == 0) || !(cond)) { \ return PT_YIELDED; \ } \ } while (0)

detaileddescription.para: Like PT_WAIT_WHILE, but returns PT_YIELDED instead of PT_WAITING, signaling the scheduler that this thread actively wants to run.

briefdescription.para: Reset the protothread and restart from PT_BEGIN on next call.

memberdef.name: PT_SPAWN
memberdef.initializer: do { \ PT_INIT(child); \ (pt)->lc = __LINE__; \ SYN_FALLTHROUGH; \ case __LINE__: \ if ((func) < PT_EXITED) { \ return PT_WAITING; \ } \ } while (0)

briefdescription.para: Spawn a child protothread and block until it exits.

detaileddescription.para:
pt
Parent protothread.
child
Child protothread (SYN_PT struct).
func
Expression that calls the child's function, e.g. child_func(&child_pt).
Usage: staticSYN_PTchild_pt; PT_SPAWN(pt,&child_pt,child_func(&child_pt));
Requires a uint32_t variable to store the deadline. When used with the scheduler, pass &(task)->delay_until. For standalone use, pass any persistent uint32_t *.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a uint32_t that will hold the deadline tick.

briefdescription.para: Convenience form for use with the scheduler's SYN_Task.

detaileddescription.para: Check if a protothread is still running (has not exited).
Check if a protothread has not yet started or has been reset.
Thread is blocked (condition not yet met)
Thread ran to PT_END — normal completion
Thread was explicitly ended via PT_EXIT

briefdescription.para: Return value from a protothread function.
Protothreads — stackless cooperative coroutines for C.

detaileddescription.para: Protothreads provide lightweight, cooperative multitasking without requiring a separate stack per thread. Each protothread is a normal C function that uses macros to save and restore its execution position via the switch/__LINE__ continuation trick (Duff's device).
A protothread costs 2 bytes of RAM (the continuation variable).

listitem.para: You cannot use a bare switch statement inside a protothread body (use if/else if chains instead).
Local variables are NOT preserved across yield/wait points. Store persistent state in static variables, globals, or a struct passed via the task's user_data pointer.
A protothread must reside entirely within a single function.
